# Demo Site Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add a `demo/` Docusaurus workspace that serves as the canonical documentation home and live demonstration of `@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs`.

**Architecture:** Yarn workspaces monorepo — the root package keeps the plugin, a new `demo/` workspace is a private Docusaurus site that imports the plugin via `workspace:*`. The FAQ page at `/faqs` is generated directly by the plugin from YAML files in `demo/data/faqs/`. The demo deploys to Cloudflare Pages via a new `deploy-demo.yml` GitHub Actions workflow. The plugin is built first; the demo site build consumes the compiled output.

**Tech Stack:** Docusaurus 3.x, React 19, TypeScript 6, Yarn 4 workspaces, Cloudflare Pages (via `cloudflare/wrangler-action@v4`), GitHub Actions.

---

## File Map

| Action | Path | Purpose |
| ------ | ---- | ------- |
| Modify | `package.json` | Add `workspaces: ["demo"]` |
| Modify | `.gitignore` | Ignore `demo/build/` and `demo/.docusaurus/` |
| Modify | `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | Add `yarn test` step with `continue-on-error: true` |
| Modify | `.github/workflows/publish.yml` | Add `packages: write` permission + `publish-gpr` job + proper npm auth |
| Create | `.github/workflows/deploy-demo.yml` | Cloudflare Pages deploy with PR previews |
| Create | `demo/package.json` | Demo workspace manifest |
| Create | `demo/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config extending `@docusaurus/tsconfig` |
| Create | `demo/sidebars.ts` | Autogenerated sidebar config |
| Create | `demo/docusaurus.config.ts` | Docusaurus site config with plugin enabled |
| Create | `demo/src/css/custom.css` | Green colour scheme + GitHub icon (copied from showcase) |
| Create | `demo/src/pages/index.tsx` | Landing page (hero + feature grid) |
| Create | `demo/src/pages/index.module.css` | Landing page layout (copied from showcase) |
| Create | `demo/static/img/favicon.ico` | Copied from showcase |
| Create | `demo/static/img/logo.svg` | Copied from showcase |
| Create | `demo/docs/intro.md` | Installation and quick start |
| Create | `demo/docs/configuration.md` | Full options reference |
| Create | `demo/docs/writing-faqs.md` | YAML format and field reference |
| Create | `demo/docs/direct-links.md` | Slugs, permalinks, auto-expand |
| Create | `demo/docs/internationalisation.md` | i18n locale fallback |
| Create | `demo/data/faqs/*.yaml` | 13 meta FAQ entries (4 categories) |
| Modify | `README.md` | Simplify to pointer toward demo site |

---

## Task 1: Repo workspace setup

**Files:**
- Modify: `package.json`
- Modify: `.gitignore`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add workspaces to root `package.json`**

  Open `package.json`. Add `"workspaces": ["demo"]` after `"publishConfig"`:

  ```json
  {
    "name": "@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "description": "A Docusaurus plugin that builds a categorised FAQ page from local YAML files",
    "type": "module",
    "main": "lib/index.js",
    "types": "lib/index.d.ts",
    "exports": {
      ".": {
        "types": "./lib/index.d.ts",
        "import": "./lib/index.js",
        "default": "./lib/index.js"
      }
    },
    "files": [
      "lib/**/*.js",
      "lib/**/*.js.map",
      "lib/**/*.d.ts",
      "lib/**/*.d.ts.map",
      "lib/**/*.css"
    ],
    "scripts": {
      "build": "tsc && copyfiles -u 1 \"src/**/*.css\" lib",
      "watch": "tsc --watch",
      "lint": "eslint src",
      "format": "prettier --write 'src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'",
      "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1"
    },
    "repository": {
      "type": "git",
      "url": "git+https://github.com/homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs.git"
    },
    "keywords": [
      "docusaurus",
      "docusaurus-plugin",
      "faq",
      "faqs"
    ],
    "author": "Mikey O'Toole <mikey@homotechsual.dev>",
    "license": "Apache-2.0",
    "bugs": {
      "url": "https://github.com/homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs/issues"
    },
    "homepage": "https://github.com/homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs#readme",
    "peerDependencies": {
      "@docusaurus/core": ">=3.0.0 <5.0.0",
      "react": "*",
      "react-dom": "*"
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "@docusaurus/utils-validation": "^3.10.1",
      "js-yaml": "^4.2.0",
      "marked": "^18.0.5"
    },
    "devDependencies": {
      "@docusaurus/core": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/module-type-aliases": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/theme-classic": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/types": "^3.10.1",
      "@types/js-yaml": "^4.0.9",
      "@types/node": "^22.19.20",
      "@types/react": "^19.2.17",
      "copyfiles": "^2.4.1",
      "eslint": "^10.4.1",
      "eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.8",
      "globals": "^17.6.0",
      "prettier": "^3.8.3",
      "react": "^19.2.7",
      "react-dom": "^19.2.7",
      "typescript": "^6.0.3",
      "typescript-eslint": "^8.60.1"
    },
    "packageManager": "yarn@4.16.0",
    "publishConfig": {
      "access": "public"
    },
    "workspaces": [
      "demo"
    ]
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update `.gitignore`**

  Append to `.gitignore`:

  ```
  # Demo site build output
  demo/build/
  demo/.docusaurus/
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add package.json .gitignore
  git commit -m "chore: add yarn workspaces for demo site"
  ```

---

## Task 2: Update existing workflows

**Files:**
- Modify: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Modify: `.github/workflows/publish.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `.github/workflows/ci.yml`**

  ```yaml
  name: CI

  on:
    push:
      branches: [main, master]
    pull_request:
      branches: [main, master]

  jobs:
    build:
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest

      steps:
        - uses: actions/checkout@v6

        - name: Remove built-in Yarn
          run: npm uninstall -g yarn

        - name: Enable Corepack
          run: corepack enable

        - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
          with:
            node-version: 'current'
            cache: 'yarn'

        - run: yarn install --immutable

        - run: yarn build

        - run: yarn lint

        - run: yarn test
          continue-on-error: true
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Replace `.github/workflows/publish.yml`**

  ```yaml
  name: Publish

  on:
    release:
      types: [published]

  permissions:
    contents: read
    id-token: write
    packages: write

  jobs:
    publish-npm:
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      environment: npm

      steps:
        - uses: actions/checkout@v6

        - name: Remove built-in Yarn
          run: npm uninstall -g yarn

        - name: Enable Corepack
          run: corepack enable

        - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
          with:
            node-version: 'current'
            cache: 'yarn'
            registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'

        - run: yarn install --immutable

        - run: yarn build

        - run: npm publish --access public
          env:
            NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

    publish-gpr:
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      needs: publish-npm

      steps:
        - uses: actions/checkout@v6

        - name: Remove built-in Yarn
          run: npm uninstall -g yarn

        - name: Enable Corepack
          run: corepack enable

        - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
          with:
            node-version: 'current'
            cache: 'yarn'
            registry-url: 'https://npm.pkg.github.com'

        - run: yarn install --immutable

        - run: yarn build

        - run: npm publish --access public
          env:
            NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/publish.yml
  git commit -m "ci: add test step to CI and GPR publishing to publish workflow"
  ```

---

## Task 3: Add deploy-demo workflow

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/deploy-demo.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `.github/workflows/deploy-demo.yml`**

  ```yaml
  name: Deploy Demo

  on:
    push:
      branches:
        - main
    pull_request:
      types:
        - opened
        - synchronize
    pull_request_target:
      types:
        - opened
        - labeled
    workflow_dispatch:

  permissions:
    contents: write
    deployments: write
    pull-requests: write

  jobs:
    deploy:
      name: Deploy
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      if: ${{
        github.event_name == 'push' ||
        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
        (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork == false) ||
        (github.event_name == 'pull_request_target' &&
        github.event.action == 'labeled' &&
        github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork == true &&
        contains(github.event.label.name, 'request-deploy'))
        }}

      concurrency:
        group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref || github.ref }}
        cancel-in-progress: true
      timeout-minutes: 15

      steps:
        - name: Checkout
          uses: actions/checkout@v6
          with:
            ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.ref }}
            fetch-depth: 0

        - name: Remove built-in Yarn
          run: npm uninstall -g yarn

        - name: Enable Corepack
          run: corepack enable

        - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
          with:
            node-version: 'current'
            cache: 'yarn'

        - name: Install dependencies
          run: yarn install --immutable

        - name: Build plugin
          run: yarn build

        - name: Build demo
          run: yarn workspace docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo build

        - name: Publish to Cloudflare Pages
          id: cloudflare-pages-deploy
          uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v4
          with:
            accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
            apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
            command: 'pages deploy demo/build --project-name=docusaurus-faqs'
            gitHubToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

        - name: Resolve deployment URLs
          id: deployment-urls
          shell: bash
          env:
            DEPLOYMENT_URL: ${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.deployment-url }}
            ALIAS_URL: ${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-deployment-alias-url }}
          run: |
            preview_url="$ALIAS_URL"
            if [ -z "$preview_url" ] || [ "$preview_url" = "undefined" ]; then
              preview_url="$DEPLOYMENT_URL"
            fi
            echo "deployment_url=$DEPLOYMENT_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "preview_url=$preview_url" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

        - name: Write deployment summary
          shell: bash
          env:
            COMMIT_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
            RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
            ENVIRONMENT: ${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-environment }}
            DEPLOYMENT_URL: ${{ steps.deployment-urls.outputs.deployment_url }}
            PREVIEW_URL: ${{ steps.deployment-urls.outputs.preview_url }}
          run: |
            {
              echo "## Deploying with Cloudflare Pages"
              echo
              echo "| Name | Result |"
              echo "| --- | --- |"
              echo "| Last commit | \`$COMMIT_SHA\` |"
              echo "| Deploy log | [View run]($RUN_URL) |"
              if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "push" ] && [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
                echo "| Deployment URL | [$DEPLOYMENT_URL]($DEPLOYMENT_URL) |"
              else
                echo "| Preview URL | [$PREVIEW_URL]($PREVIEW_URL) |"
                echo "| Deployment URL | [$DEPLOYMENT_URL]($DEPLOYMENT_URL) |"
              fi
              echo "| Environment | $ENVIRONMENT |"
            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

        - name: Create commit comment
          if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}
          uses: peter-evans/commit-comment@v4
          with:
            body: |
              ### Successfully deployed to Cloudflare Pages!

              | Item | Value |
              | --- | --- |
              | **Latest commit** | ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }} |
              | **Deploy log** | ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }} |
              | **Deployment URL** | [${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.deployment-url }}](${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.deployment-url }}) |
              | **Environment** | ${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-environment }} |

        - name: Create PR comment
          if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' || github.event_name == 'pull_request_target' }}
          uses: mshick/add-pr-comment@v2
          with:
            message: |
              ### Successfully deployed to Cloudflare Pages!

              | Item | Value |
              | --- | --- |
              | **Latest commit** | ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }} |
              | **Deploy log** | ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }} |
              | **Preview URL** | [${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-deployment-alias-url || steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.deployment-url }}](${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-deployment-alias-url || steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.deployment-url }}) |
              | **Environment** | ${{ steps.cloudflare-pages-deploy.outputs.pages-environment }} |

    comment:
      name: Comment
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      if: ${{
        github.event_name == 'pull_request_target' &&
        github.event.action == 'opened' &&
        github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork == true
        }}

      steps:
        - name: Create PR comment
          run: |
            cat << EOF > comment.md
            ## Deployments require the 'request-deploy' label

            This is a forked repository. For security reasons, deployments from forks are not automatic.
            Add the 'request-deploy' label to this PR to trigger a preview deployment.
            EOF
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.number }} -R ${{ github.repository }} -F comment.md
          env:
            GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add .github/workflows/deploy-demo.yml
  git commit -m "ci: add Cloudflare Pages deploy-demo workflow"
  ```

---

## Task 4: Demo package scaffolding

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/package.json`
- Create: `demo/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `demo/sidebars.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `demo/package.json`**

  ```json
  {
    "name": "docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo",
    "version": "0.0.0",
    "private": true,
    "scripts": {
      "start": "docusaurus start",
      "build": "docusaurus build",
      "serve": "docusaurus serve",
      "clear": "docusaurus clear"
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "@docusaurus/core": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/preset-classic": "^3.10.1",
      "@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs": "workspace:*",
      "react": "^19.2.7",
      "react-dom": "^19.2.7"
    },
    "devDependencies": {
      "@docusaurus/module-type-aliases": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/tsconfig": "^3.10.1",
      "@docusaurus/types": "^3.10.1",
      "typescript": "^6.0.3"
    }
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `demo/tsconfig.json`**

  ```json
  {
    "extends": "@docusaurus/tsconfig",
    "compilerOptions": {
      "baseUrl": "."
    }
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `demo/sidebars.ts`**

  ```ts
  import type { SidebarsConfig } from '@docusaurus/plugin-content-docs'

  const sidebars: SidebarsConfig = {
    docs: [{ type: 'autogenerated', dirName: '.' }],
  }

  export default sidebars
  ```

- [ ] **Step 4: Install dependencies from repo root**

  ```bash
  yarn install
  ```

  Expected: Yarn resolves the new `demo/` workspace, installs Docusaurus and its dependencies, links `@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs` to the local `workspace:*`, and updates `yarn.lock`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/package.json demo/tsconfig.json demo/sidebars.ts yarn.lock
  git commit -m "feat(demo): scaffold demo workspace"
  ```

---

## Task 5: Demo Docusaurus config

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/docusaurus.config.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `demo/docusaurus.config.ts`**

  ```ts
  import { themes as prismThemes } from 'prism-react-renderer'
  import type { Config } from '@docusaurus/types'
  import type * as Preset from '@docusaurus/preset-classic'
  import { DOCUSAURUS_VERSION } from '@docusaurus/utils'

  const config: Config = {
    title: 'FAQs for Docusaurus',
    tagline: 'Build a categorised FAQ page from YAML files',
    favicon: 'img/favicon.ico',
    url: 'https://faqs.docusaurus.homotechsual.dev',
    baseUrl: '/',
    onBrokenLinks: 'warn',
    onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'warn',
    i18n: {
      defaultLocale: 'en',
      locales: ['en'],
    },
    plugins: [
      [
        '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
        {
          path: 'data/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'faqs',
        },
      ],
    ],
    presets: [
      [
        'classic',
        {
          docs: {
            sidebarPath: './sidebars.ts',
          },
          blog: false,
          theme: {
            customCss: './src/css/custom.css',
          },
        } satisfies Preset.Options,
      ],
    ],
    themeConfig: {
      navbar: {
        title: 'FAQs Plugin',
        items: [
          {
            type: 'docSidebar',
            sidebarId: 'docs',
            position: 'left',
            label: 'Docs',
          },
          {
            to: '/faqs',
            label: 'FAQ Demo',
            position: 'left',
          },
          {
            href: 'https://github.com/homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
            label: 'GitHub',
            position: 'right',
            className: 'github-link',
          },
        ],
      },
      footer: {
        style: 'dark',
        links: [
          {
            title: 'Documentation',
            items: [
              { label: 'Introduction', to: '/docs/intro' },
              { label: 'Configuration', to: '/docs/configuration' },
              { label: 'Writing FAQ Entries', to: '/docs/writing-faqs' },
              { label: 'Direct Links', to: '/docs/direct-links' },
              { label: 'Internationalisation', to: '/docs/internationalisation' },
            ],
          },
          {
            title: 'Links',
            items: [
              {
                label: 'GitHub',
                href: 'https://github.com/homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
              },
              {
                label: 'npm',
                href: 'https://www.npmjs.com/package/@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
              },
            ],
          },
        ],
        copyright: `Copyright © ${new Date().getFullYear()} Mikey O'Toole.<br />Built with <a href="https://docusaurus.io">Docusaurus v${DOCUSAURUS_VERSION}</a>.<br /><span class="designedBy">Designed with <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="heart" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M14 20.408c-.492.308-.903.546-1.192.709-.153.086-.308.17-.463.252h-.002a.75.75 0 01-.686 0 16.709 16.709 0 01-.465-.252 31.147 31.147 0 01-4.803-3.34C3.8 15.572 1 12.331 1 8.513 1 5.052 3.829 2.5 6.736 2.5 9.03 2.5 10.881 3.726 12 5.605 13.12 3.726 14.97 2.5 17.264 2.5 20.17 2.5 23 5.052 23 8.514c0 3.818-2.801 7.06-5.389 9.262A31.146 31.146 0 0114 20.408z"/></svg> by <a href="https://homotechsual.dev">homotechsual</a></span>`,
      },
      prism: {
        theme: prismThemes.github,
        darkTheme: prismThemes.dracula,
      },
      future: {
        faster: true,
        v4: true,
      },
      storage: {
        type: 'localStorage',
        namespace: true,
      },
    } satisfies Preset.ThemeConfig,
  }

  export default config
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/docusaurus.config.ts
  git commit -m "feat(demo): add Docusaurus config"
  ```

---

## Task 6: Static assets and CSS

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/src/css/custom.css`
- Create: `demo/src/pages/index.module.css`
- Create: `demo/static/img/favicon.ico`
- Create: `demo/static/img/logo.svg`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create directory structure**

  ```bash
  mkdir -p demo/src/css
  mkdir -p demo/src/pages
  mkdir -p demo/static/img
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Copy `custom.css` from showcase**

  Copy `J:\Projects\docusaurus-plugin-showcase\demo\src\css\custom.css` verbatim to `demo/src/css/custom.css`. The file uses the green colour scheme (`#2e8555` light / `#25c2a0` dark) and includes inline base64 GitHub and Discord icon styles — copy without modification.

  The file content is:

  ```css
  :root {
    --ifm-color-primary: #2e8555;
    --ifm-color-primary-dark: #29784c;
    --ifm-color-primary-darker: #277148;
    --ifm-color-primary-darkest: #205d3b;
    --ifm-color-primary-light: #33925d;
    --ifm-color-primary-lighter: #359962;
    --ifm-color-primary-lightest: #3cad6e;
    --ifm-code-font-size: 95%;
    --site-color-favourite-background: #f6d5f7;
    --site-color-svg-icon-favourite: #e9669e;
    --site-color-checkbox-checked-bg: hsl(167deg 56% 73% / 25%);
  }

  [data-theme='dark'] {
    --ifm-color-primary: #25c2a0;
    --ifm-color-primary-dark: #21af90;
    --ifm-color-primary-darker: #1fa588;
    --ifm-color-primary-lightest: #4fddbf;
    --site-color-favourite-background: #4a1d4a;
    --site-color-checkbox-checked-bg: hsl(167deg 56% 73% / 10%);
  }

  @keyframes heart {
    0%, 40%, 80%, 100% {
      transform: scale(1);
    }
    20%, 60% {
      transform: scale(1.15);
    }
  }

  .designedBy {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;

    .heart {
      margin: 0.25rem;
      animation: heart 1500ms infinite;
      fill: red;
    }

    a {
      margin-inline-start: 0.25rem;
    }
  }

  html[data-theme='dark'] .github-link {
    align-items: center;
    display: flex;

    &:before {
      align-self: center;
      background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      content: '';
      display: inline-flex;
      height: 24px;
      width: 24px;
      margin-right: 0.5rem;
      color: var(--ifm-navbar-link-color);
    }

    &:hover {
      &:before {
        background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      }
    }
  }

  html[data-theme='light'] .github-link {
    align-items: center;
    display: flex;

    &:before {
      align-self: center;
      background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      content: '';
      display: inline-flex;
      height: 24px;
      width: 24px;
      margin-right: 0.5rem;
      color: var(--ifm-navbar-link-color);
    }

    &:hover {
      &:before {
        background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      }
    }
  }

  html[data-theme='dark'] .discord-link {
    align-items: center;
    display: flex;

    &:before {
      align-self: center;
      background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      content: '';
      display: inline-flex;
      height: 24px;
      width: 24px;
      margin-right: 0.5rem;
      color: var(--ifm-navbar-link-color);
    }

    &:hover {
      &:before {
        background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      }
    }
  }

  html[data-theme='light'] .discord-link {
    align-items: center;
    display: flex;

    &:before {
      align-self: center;
      background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      content: '';
      display: inline-flex;
      height: 24px;
      width: 24px;
      margin-right: 0.5rem;
      color: var(--ifm-navbar-link-color);
    }

    &:hover {
      &:before {
        background: url("data:image/svg+xml;base64,") 0 0/contain;
      }
    }
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `demo/src/pages/index.module.css`**

  ```css
  .hero {
    padding: 4rem 0;
    text-align: center;
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
  }

  .buttons {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    gap: 1rem;
    margin-top: 1.5rem;
  }

  .features {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    padding: 2rem 0;
    width: 100%;
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 4: Copy binary assets from showcase**

  ```bash
  cp "J:\Projects\docusaurus-plugin-showcase\demo\static\img\favicon.ico" demo/static/img/favicon.ico
  cp "J:\Projects\docusaurus-plugin-showcase\demo\static\img\logo.svg" demo/static/img/logo.svg
  ```

  On Linux/CI (if running this task there, copy the files locally first):

  The files must be present before the build runs. The `.ico` is a binary — do not attempt to create it manually.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/src/css/custom.css demo/src/pages/index.module.css demo/static/
  git commit -m "feat(demo): add CSS theme and static assets"
  ```

---

## Task 7: Documentation pages

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/docs/intro.md`
- Create: `demo/docs/configuration.md`
- Create: `demo/docs/writing-faqs.md`
- Create: `demo/docs/direct-links.md`
- Create: `demo/docs/internationalisation.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `demo/docs/intro.md`**

  ```md
  ---
  sidebar_position: 1
  ---

  # Introduction

  **FAQs for Docusaurus** builds a categorised, collapsible FAQ page from a directory of local YAML files. Add the plugin, write some YAML, and you get a fully generated page at the URL of your choosing — no React, no MDX, no database.

  ## Installation

  ```bash
  npm install @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
  # or
  yarn add @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
  ```

  ## Quick start

  Add the plugin to your `docusaurus.config.ts`:

  ```ts
  import faqsPlugin from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'
  import type { PluginOptions as FAQOptions } from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'

  export default {
    plugins: [
      [
        faqsPlugin,
        {
          path: 'data/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'faqs',
        } satisfies FAQOptions,
      ],
    ],
  }
  ```

  Or in `docusaurus.config.js`:

  ```js
  export default {
    plugins: [
      [
        '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
        {
          path: 'data/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'faqs',
        },
      ],
    ],
  }
  ```

  Then create `data/faqs/my-first-faq.yaml` in your site directory:

  ```yaml
  question: What does this plugin do?
  answer: It builds a categorised FAQ page from local YAML files.
  category: General
  order: 1
  ```

  Run `docusaurus build` (or `docusaurus start`) and visit `/faqs`.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `demo/docs/configuration.md`**

  ```md
  ---
  sidebar_position: 2
  ---

  # Configuration

  All options are passed as the second element of the plugin tuple in your `docusaurus.config.ts` (or `.js`).

  ## Options

  | Option | Type | Required | Default | Description |
  | ------ | ---- | -------- | ------- | ----------- |
  | `path` | `string` | No | `"data/faqs"` | Path to the directory containing your `*.yaml`/`*.yml` FAQ files. Relative paths are resolved from your site directory; absolute paths are used as-is. |
  | `routeBasePath` | `string` | No | `"faqs"` | URL path where the generated FAQ page is served. |

  ## TypeScript example

  ```ts
  import faqsPlugin from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'
  import type { PluginOptions as FAQOptions } from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'

  export default {
    plugins: [
      [
        faqsPlugin,
        {
          path: 'content/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'help',
        } satisfies FAQOptions,
      ],
    ],
  }
  ```

  This serves the FAQ page at `/help` and reads YAML files from `content/faqs/`.

  ## JavaScript example

  ```js
  export default {
    plugins: [
      [
        '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs',
        {
          path: 'content/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'help',
        },
      ],
    ],
  }
  ```
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `demo/docs/writing-faqs.md`**

  ```md
  ---
  sidebar_position: 3
  ---

  # Writing FAQ Entries

  Each FAQ entry is a single YAML file inside the configured `path` directory. Both `.yaml` and `.yml` extensions are recognised. Files are read in alphabetical order by filename.

  ## File format

  ```yaml
  question: How do I reset my password?
  answer: |
    Open the **sign in** page and select *Forgot password*. You'll receive
    an email with a link to choose a new one.
  category: Accounts
  order: 1
  ```

  ## Field reference

  | Field | Type | Required | Description |
  | ----- | ---- | -------- | ----------- |
  | `question` | `string` | Yes | The question text, rendered as plain text |
  | `answer` | `string` | Yes | The answer, rendered as Markdown (CommonMark via [marked](https://github.com/markedjs/marked)) |
  | `category` | `string` | No | Groups related questions under a heading. Entries without a category appear under **General** and are sorted last |
  | `order` | `number` | No | Controls sort order within a category — lower numbers appear first. Entries without an explicit order are sorted alphabetically by question, after any explicitly ordered entries |

  ## Sorting behaviour

  1. Categories are sorted alphabetically.
  2. Within a category, entries with an explicit `order` come first (ascending).
  3. Remaining entries within a category are sorted alphabetically by `question`.
  4. Entries without a `category` are collected under **General** and placed after all named categories.

  ## Skip behaviour

  Files that fail YAML parsing, or that are missing a string `question` or `answer` field, are skipped with a warning printed to the console at build time. The build continues — invalid files do not cause a build failure.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create `demo/docs/direct-links.md`**

  ```md
  ---
  sidebar_position: 4
  ---

  # Direct Links

  Every FAQ entry gets a stable anchor that can be shared as a deep link to that specific question.

  ## How slugs are derived

  The plugin converts the `question` text to a URL-safe slug:

  1. Convert to lowercase
  2. Strip characters that are not alphanumeric, whitespace, or hyphens
  3. Trim leading and trailing whitespace
  4. Replace spaces and underscores with hyphens
  5. Collapse runs of hyphens to a single hyphen

  For example: `"How do I reset my password?"` → `how-do-i-reset-my-password`

  ## Duplicate slug disambiguation

  If two questions produce the same slug, the second gets a `-2` suffix, the third `-3`, and so on. Slugs are assigned in file read order (alphabetical by filename).

  ## The permalink icon

  Each question row shows a `#` link on hover. Clicking it copies the URL fragment to the browser address bar and adds it to the browser history.

  ## Auto-expand on load

  When a visitor lands on the FAQ page with a fragment in the URL (e.g. `/faqs#how-do-i-reset-my-password`), the matching entry is automatically expanded and scrolled into view. This also works when navigating between pages within the same Docusaurus site.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 5: Create `demo/docs/internationalisation.md`**

  ```md
  ---
  sidebar_position: 5
  ---

  # Internationalisation

  The plugin integrates with Docusaurus's built-in i18n system. When building a non-default locale, it looks for translated FAQ files in a locale-specific directory before falling back to the configured `path`.

  ## Locale directory

  For a locale named `fr`, the plugin checks:

  ```
  i18n/fr/docusaurus-plugin-faqs/
  ```

  If that directory exists, it is used **instead of** the configured `path`. If it does not exist, the plugin falls back to the configured `path` (the default-locale files).

  ## Setup

  1. Enable your locale in `docusaurus.config.ts`:

     ```ts
     i18n: {
       defaultLocale: 'en',
       locales: ['en', 'fr'],
     }
     ```

  2. Create the locale directory and add translated YAML files:

     ```
     i18n/
     └── fr/
         └── docusaurus-plugin-faqs/
             ├── my-first-faq.yaml
             └── my-second-faq.yaml
     ```

  3. Build the French locale:

     ```bash
     docusaurus build --locale fr
     ```

  The translated FAQ page is served at the locale's URL prefix (e.g. `/fr/faqs`).

  ## Partial translations

  If the locale directory exists but contains fewer files than the default, only those files are shown. No merging with the default-locale files occurs — the locale directory fully replaces the configured `path` when it exists.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify docs build**

  From the repo root, build the plugin then the demo:

  ```bash
  yarn build && yarn workspace docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo build
  ```

  Expected: no errors. The build output appears in `demo/build/`. If TypeScript errors appear in `demo/docusaurus.config.ts`, fix them before continuing.

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/docs/
  git commit -m "feat(demo): add documentation pages"
  ```

---

## Task 8: FAQ YAML content

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/data/faqs/` (13 YAML files)

The plugin sorts categories alphabetically, so the rendered order is: **Features → Getting Started → Troubleshooting → Writing FAQs**. Explicit `order` fields control the order within each category.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `demo/data/faqs/features-01-direct-links.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: How do direct links work?
  category: Features
  order: 1
  answer: |
    Every FAQ entry is assigned a stable slug derived from the question text (e.g. "How do I reset my password?" → `how-do-i-reset-my-password`). A `#` permalink appears next to each question on hover.

    Visiting the page with a matching URL fragment (e.g. `/faqs#how-do-i-reset-my-password`) automatically expands that entry and scrolls it into view. See [Direct Links](/docs/direct-links) for the full slugification rules and how duplicate slugs are handled.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `demo/data/faqs/features-02-markdown.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: What Markdown is supported in answers?
  category: Features
  order: 2
  answer: |
    Answers are parsed with [marked](https://github.com/markedjs/marked), which implements the CommonMark spec. All standard Markdown is supported: **bold**, *italic*, `inline code`, links, lists, blockquotes, and fenced code blocks.

    HTML is rendered as-is if you include it. Docusaurus MDX features (admonitions, React components) are not available — plain Markdown only.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `demo/data/faqs/features-03-i18n.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: Does the plugin work with Docusaurus i18n?
  category: Features
  order: 3
  answer: |
    Yes. When building a non-default locale, the plugin looks for FAQ files in `i18n/<locale>/docusaurus-plugin-faqs/`. If that directory exists it is used in place of the configured `path`; otherwise the default-locale files are used as a fallback.

    See [Internationalisation](/docs/internationalisation) for step-by-step setup instructions.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create `demo/data/faqs/getting-started-01-install.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: How do I install the plugin?
  category: Getting Started
  order: 1
  answer: |
    Install the package from npm:

    ```bash
    npm install @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
    # or
    yarn add @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
    ```

    See [Introduction](/docs/intro) for the full quick-start guide including the minimal Docusaurus config.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 5: Create `demo/data/faqs/getting-started-02-config.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: How do I add the plugin to my Docusaurus config?
  category: Getting Started
  order: 2
  answer: |
    Add it to the `plugins` array in `docusaurus.config.ts`:

    ```ts
    import faqsPlugin from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'

    export default {
      plugins: [
        [faqsPlugin, { routeBasePath: 'faqs' }],
      ],
    }
    ```

    See [Introduction](/docs/intro) for the full TypeScript and JavaScript examples, or [Configuration](/docs/configuration) for all available options.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 6: Create `demo/data/faqs/getting-started-03-where-to-put-files.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: Where do I put my FAQ files?
  category: Getting Started
  order: 3
  answer: |
    By default the plugin reads `*.yaml` and `*.yml` files from `data/faqs/` relative to your site directory. You can change this with the `path` option:

    ```ts
    [faqsPlugin, { path: 'content/faqs' }]
    ```

    See [Configuration](/docs/configuration) for the full options reference.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 7: Create `demo/data/faqs/troubleshooting-01-file-skipped.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: My FAQ file is being skipped — why?
  category: Troubleshooting
  order: 1
  answer: |
    Two things cause a file to be skipped, both logged as warnings at build time:

    1. **Parse error** — the file is not valid YAML. Check for indentation mistakes, unquoted special characters, or a missing `|` on a multi-line answer.
    2. **Missing required fields** — every entry must have a string `question` and a string `answer`. If either is absent or the wrong type the entry is skipped.

    See [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs) for the correct file format and field reference.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 8: Create `demo/data/faqs/troubleshooting-02-all-general.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: All my questions appear under "General" — what's wrong?
  category: Troubleshooting
  order: 2
  answer: |
    This happens when the `category` field is missing or not a string. Common causes:

    - The field is named `categories` (plural) instead of `category`
    - The value is a number or list rather than a plain string
    - The field is indented under `answer` by mistake

    Check each file against the format in [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs).
  ```

- [ ] **Step 9: Create `demo/data/faqs/troubleshooting-03-empty-page.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: The FAQ page is empty but my YAML files exist
  category: Troubleshooting
  order: 3
  answer: |
    The plugin is probably looking in the wrong directory. Check two things:

    1. **`path` option** — the default is `data/faqs` relative to your site directory. If your files are elsewhere, set `path` in the plugin config. See [Configuration](/docs/configuration).
    2. **File extensions** — only `.yaml` and `.yml` files are read. Files with other extensions (`.yml.bak`, `.txt`) are silently ignored.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 10: Create `demo/data/faqs/writing-faqs-01-fields.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: What fields does a FAQ entry support?
  category: Writing FAQs
  order: 1
  answer: |
    Each YAML file supports four fields:

    - **`question`** *(required)* — the question text, rendered as plain text
    - **`answer`** *(required)* — the answer, rendered as Markdown
    - **`category`** *(optional)* — groups questions under a heading; defaults to **General**
    - **`order`** *(optional)* — controls sort order within a category; lower numbers appear first

    See [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs) for the full field reference and a complete example file.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 11: Create `demo/data/faqs/writing-faqs-02-categories.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: How do I group questions into categories?
  category: Writing FAQs
  order: 2
  answer: |
    Add a `category` field to your YAML file:

    ```yaml
    question: How do I reset my password?
    answer: Visit the sign-in page and select *Forgot password*.
    category: Account Management
    ```

    Questions sharing the same `category` value are grouped under a heading. Categories are sorted alphabetically. See [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs) for sorting details.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 12: Create `demo/data/faqs/writing-faqs-03-ordering.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: How do I control the order of entries within a category?
  category: Writing FAQs
  order: 3
  answer: |
    Add an `order` field (a number). Lower numbers appear first:

    ```yaml
    question: First question in the category
    answer: ...
    category: My Category
    order: 1
    ```

    Entries without an explicit `order` come after all ordered entries and are sorted alphabetically by question. See [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs) for the full sorting rules.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 13: Create `demo/data/faqs/writing-faqs-04-no-category.yaml`**

  ```yaml
  question: What happens if I omit the category field?
  category: Writing FAQs
  order: 4
  answer: |
    Entries without a `category` are grouped under a heading labelled **General** and sorted after all named categories. If every entry on the page is uncategorised the heading is omitted entirely — only one section appears with no heading.

    See [Writing FAQ Entries](/docs/writing-faqs) for details on sorting behaviour.
  ```

- [ ] **Step 14: Verify build with FAQ content**

  ```bash
  yarn build && yarn workspace docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo build
  ```

  Expected: no errors. `demo/build/faqs/index.html` is generated.

- [ ] **Step 15: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/data/
  git commit -m "feat(demo): add meta FAQ YAML content"
  ```

---

## Task 9: Landing page

**Files:**
- Create: `demo/src/pages/index.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `demo/src/pages/index.tsx`**

  ```tsx
  import React from 'react'
  import clsx from 'clsx'
  import Link from '@docusaurus/Link'
  import useDocusaurusContext from '@docusaurus/useDocusaurusContext'
  import Layout from '@theme/Layout'
  import Heading from '@theme/Heading'
  import styles from './index.module.css'

  const features = [
    {
      title: 'YAML-driven',
      description:
        'Write questions and answers in plain YAML — no React, no MDX, no database. One file per entry.',
    },
    {
      title: 'Categories & ordering',
      description:
        'Group questions under headings with the category field. Control the order within each group explicitly with order, or let them sort alphabetically.',
    },
    {
      title: 'Direct links',
      description:
        'Every question gets a stable permalink derived from the question text. Visiting the URL automatically expands that entry and scrolls it into view.',
    },
    {
      title: 'i18n-ready',
      description:
        'Drop translated YAML files into i18n/<locale>/docusaurus-plugin-faqs/ and the plugin picks them up automatically for non-default locale builds.',
    },
  ]

  function Feature({ title, description }: { title: string; description: string }) {
    return (
      <div className={clsx('col col--3')}>
        <div className="padding-horiz--md">
          <Heading as="h3">{title}</Heading>
          <p>{description}</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  function HomepageFeatures() {
    return (
      <section className={styles.features}>
        <div className="container">
          <div className="row">
            {features.map((props, idx) => (
              <Feature key={idx} {...props} />
            ))}
          </div>
        </div>
      </section>
    )
  }

  function HomepageHero() {
    const { siteConfig } = useDocusaurusContext()
    return (
      <header className={clsx('hero hero--primary', styles.hero)}>
        <div className="container">
          <Heading as="h1" className="hero__title">
            {siteConfig.title}
          </Heading>
          <p className="hero__subtitle">{siteConfig.tagline}</p>
          <div className={styles.buttons}>
            <Link className="button button--secondary button--lg" to="/faqs">
              View the FAQ Demo →
            </Link>
            <Link className="button button--outline button--secondary button--lg" to="/docs/intro">
              Read the Docs
            </Link>
          </div>
        </div>
      </header>
    )
  }

  export default function Home(): React.JSX.Element {
    const { siteConfig } = useDocusaurusContext()
    return (
      <Layout title={siteConfig.title} description={siteConfig.tagline}>
        <HomepageHero />
        <main>
          <HomepageFeatures />
        </main>
      </Layout>
    )
  }
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify build**

  ```bash
  yarn build && yarn workspace docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo build
  ```

  Expected: build succeeds. `demo/build/index.html` is generated.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add demo/src/pages/index.tsx
  git commit -m "feat(demo): add landing page"
  ```

---

## Task 10: Simplify README

**Files:**
- Modify: `README.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `README.md` with simplified version**

  ```md
  # FAQs for Docusaurus

  [![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40homotechsual%2Fdocusaurus-plugin-faqs?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs)
  [![NPM Last Update](https://img.shields.io/npm/last-update/%40homotechsual%2Fdocusaurus-plugin-faqs?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs)
  [![NPM Downloads](https://img.shields.io/npm/dy/%40homotechsual%2Fdocusaurus-plugin-faqs?style=for-the-badge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs)

  A Docusaurus plugin that builds a categorised FAQ page from local YAML files.

  **[Full documentation →](https://faqs.docusaurus.homotechsual.dev)**

  ## Installation

  ```bash
  npm install @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
  # or
  yarn add @homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs
  ```

  ## Quick start

  ```ts
  import faqsPlugin from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'
  import type { PluginOptions as FAQOptions } from '@homotechsual/docusaurus-plugin-faqs'

  export default {
    plugins: [
      [
        faqsPlugin,
        {
          path: 'data/faqs',
          routeBasePath: 'faqs',
        } satisfies FAQOptions,
      ],
    ],
  }
  ```

  For all configuration options, writing FAQ entries, direct links, and i18n support, see the [docs](https://faqs.docusaurus.homotechsual.dev).

  ## Licence

  Apache-2.0
  ```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

  ```bash
  git add README.md
  git commit -m "docs: simplify README to pointer toward demo site"
  ```

---

## Task 11: Final build verification

- [ ] **Step 1: Run full build from clean state**

  ```bash
  yarn build && yarn workspace docusaurus-plugin-faqs-demo build
  ```

  Expected final lines include:
  ```
  [SUCCESS] Generated static files in "build".
  ```

  If TypeScript errors appear, fix them before continuing.

- [ ] **Step 2: Spot-check build output**

  Confirm these files exist:

  ```bash
  ls demo/build/index.html
  ls demo/build/faqs/index.html
  ls demo/build/docs/intro/index.html
  ls demo/build/docs/configuration/index.html
  ls demo/build/docs/writing-faqs/index.html
  ls demo/build/docs/direct-links/index.html
  ls demo/build/docs/internationalisation/index.html
  ```

- [ ] **Step 3: Check lint still passes**

  ```bash
  yarn lint
  ```

  Expected: no errors or warnings from the plugin source (`src/`). The demo site files are not covered by the root ESLint config.

- [ ] **Step 4: Confirm no accidental build artifact commits**

  ```bash
  git status
  ```

  Expected: working tree clean. `demo/build/` and `demo/.docusaurus/` should be listed as ignored in `.gitignore` and not appear as untracked.

---

## Deployment prerequisites (manual — not code changes)

Before the `deploy-demo.yml` workflow will succeed on push to `main`, complete these steps in the Cloudflare and GitHub dashboards:

1. Create a Cloudflare Pages project named `docusaurus-faqs`
2. Set the custom domain `faqs.docusaurus.homotechsual.dev` in that project
3. Add `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` and `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` as secrets in the GitHub repo settings (same values as the other homotechsual plugin repos)
